expect: Test severity scoring will reveal that <20% of experiments have medium+ severity (Popperian corroboration gap from L-1390 gap #2). science_quality.py has no severity dimension.
actual: CONFIRMED. Mean test severity 0.116/1.0. Distribution: 69.8% zero, 25.8% low, 4.2% medium, 0.1% high. 95% of experiments fail Popper's demarcation criterion for severe testing. science_quality.py now includes test_severity dimension.
diff: Expected <20% medium+ severity, measured 4.3% — worse than predicted. The swarm does falsification design (23.9% of experiments) but almost never with quantitatively narrow predictions. Confirmation through vague prediction is structurally identical to confirmation bias.
external_grounding: Popper 1963 (corroboration = severe testing), Mayo 1996 (error statistics — test severity as core of scientific inference)
f_epis1_gap_addressed: gap #2 (Popper-Bayesian conflation) — science_quality.py now distinguishes confirmation (evidence exists) from corroboration (survived severe test)
